meaning: adj.坚硬的,不能弯曲的,不屈的
examples: The authorities proved unyielding on one crucial demand.
Those are blunt, unyielding admonitions that ring in my head every day.
I did not grow up with dogs, which meant that my older daughter's unyielding determination to get one required some adjustment on my part.
The Republicans seemed completely intransigent on taxes and Democrats seemed unyielding on cuts to entitlements.
This belief comes from my unyielding faith in the decency and generosity of the American people.
